name: UN Security Council 1945 (UNSC under UN Charter Chapter V)
summary: The United Nations Security Council (UNSC) is a global supranational meta-framework foundational-UN-Charter-Chapter-V framework established by the UN Charter — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating on formal international-peace-and-security framework + foundational collective-security framework with five-permanent-members-veto framework (UNSC established 24 October 1945 by UN Charter Chapter V Article 23 framework + UNSC operations began 17 January 1946 in London at first UNSC meeting + UNSC composition 15 members: 5 permanent P5 + 10 non-permanent E10 framework — formal foundational UNSC framework establishing comprehensive UNSC organisation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-treaty frameworks which operate on different substantive areas, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating on formal international-peace-and-security framework + foundational collective-security framework with five-permanent-members-veto framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal binding-resolutions framework under UN Charter Chapter VII framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal P5 Permanent Five US + UK + France + Russia + China veto framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal China-PRC-replaced-ROC-Taiwan 1971 framework via UN General Assembly Resolution 2758 framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal Russia-vetoes 2022-2026 framework over Russia-Ukraine framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal US-vetoes-Gaza-ceasefire-2023-2026 framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal UNSC-reform-Pact-for-Future 2024 framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal Veto-Initiative-2022 framework via UN General Assembly Resolution 76/262 framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal Article 27(3) procedural-substantive distinction framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal Korea peacekeeping-since-1950-framework with no-formal-end-state framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal Article 51 self-defense framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal Pillar 3 Responsibility to Protect R2P 2005 framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal UN Charter Chapter VII binding-resolutions framework (UN Charter Chapter VII Article 39-51 framework providing formal UNSC binding-resolutions framework via Article 41 sanctions + Article 42 military-action framework — formal foundational UN Charter Chapter VII binding-resolutions framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal binding-resolutions framework under UN Charter Chapter VII framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal P5 Permanent Five veto framework (UN Charter Article 27(3) framework providing formal P5 Permanent Five US + UK + France + Russia + China veto framework — formal foundational P5 veto framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal P5 Permanent Five veto framework + most-vetoes-cast: Russia/Soviet Union 158 + US 89 + UK 32 + China 21 + France 18 as of 2026 framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal UNSC reform + Pact for Future 2024 framework (Pact for the Future adopted 22 September 2024 at UN Summit of the Future New York including UNSC reform commitments + Africa-Group-Common-African-Position Ezulwini Consensus framework + G4-Brazil-Germany-India-Japan reform proposal + Uniting-for-Consensus-Italy-Pakistan-Argentina-Mexico-South-Korea reform proposal framework — formal foundational UNSC reform + Pact for Future framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal 80-year-reform-debate framework). The UNSC is operating upon 193 UN member states framework — comprehensive universal coverage framework. The Council is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal UNSC Children and Armed Conflict CAAC framework via UN Security Council Resolution 1612 of 26 July 2005 operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Six Grave Violations Against Children framework + UN Secretary-General Annual Report on Children and Armed Conflict framework + UN Security Council Working Group on Children and Armed Conflict framework. The UNSC + UNSC Subsidiary Bodies including Sanctions Committees + Counter-Terrorism Committee CTC + Counter-Terrorism Executive Directorate CTED + Working Group on Children and Armed Conflict are the formal UNSC bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The UNSC is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.citation: UN Charter Chapter V Article 23 (UNSC Composition Framework)
statutory_framework.title: UN Charter Chapter V Article 23
statutory_framework.relevance: UN Charter Chapter V Article 23 framework on UNSC composition: 15 members 5 permanent + 10 non-permanent — formal foundational UNSC composition framework.

statutory_framework.citation: UN Charter Chapter VII Articles 39-51 (Binding-Resolutions Framework)
statutory_framework.title: UN Charter Chapter VII Articles 39-51
statutory_framework.relevance: UN Charter Chapter VII Articles 39-51 framework on UNSC binding-resolutions: Article 41 sanctions + Article 42 military-action framework — formal foundational UN Charter Chapter VII binding-resolutions framework.

statutory_framework.title: UN Charter Article 27(3)
statutory_framework.relevance: UN Charter Article 27(3) framework on P5 Permanent Five US + UK + France + Russia + China veto — formal foundational P5 veto framework + procedural-substantive distinction framework.

statutory_framework.title: UN General Assembly Resolution 2758
statutory_framework.relevance: UN General Assembly Resolution 2758 of 25 October 1971 — formal China-PRC-replaced-ROC-Taiwan framework.

statutory_framework.citation: UN Security Council Resolution 1612 (2005) Children and Armed Conflict
statutory_framework.title: UN Security Council Resolution 1612
statutory_framework.relevance: UN Security Council Resolution 1612 of 26 July 2005 — formal foundational Children and Armed Conflict Six Grave Violations Against Children framework.

statutory_framework.title: Pact for the Future
statutory_framework.relevance: Pact for the Future adopted 22 September 2024 at UN Summit of the Future New York — formal foundational Pact for the Future + UNSC reform commitments framework.

statutory_framework.citation: UN General Assembly Resolution 76/262 (2022) Veto Initiative
statutory_framework.title: UN General Assembly Resolution 76/262
statutory_framework.relevance: UN General Assembly Resolution 76/262 of 26 April 2022 — formal Veto Initiative framework requiring UN General Assembly debate on every veto.

apex_courts.name: UN Security Council
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for UNSC — composed of 15 members: 5 permanent P5 US + UK + France + Russia + China + 10 non-permanent E10 elected by UN General Assembly for 2-year terms.

apex_courts.name: UNSC Sanctions Committees (15+ committees)
apex_courts.role: Formal UNSC Sanctions Committees subsidiary bodies.

apex_courts.name: UN Counter-Terrorism Committee (CTC) + Counter-Terrorism Executive Directorate (CTED)
apex_courts.role: Formal UNSC counter-terrorism subsidiary bodies established by UN Security Council Resolution 1373 of 28 September 2001 + CTED 2004.

apex_courts.name: UN Security Council Working Group on Children and Armed Conflict
apex_courts.role: Formal UNSC Working Group on Children and Armed Conflict body established by UN Security Council Resolution 1612 of 26 July 2005.

professional_regulators.name: UN Department of Political and Peacebuilding Affairs DPPA

anonymisation_convention: UNSC-related communications operate via state-state framework via UNSC members; individual communications not formally accepted under UNSC framework.

key_developments.title: UNSC established + UN Charter
key_developments.description: UN Charter signed 26 June 1945 in San Francisco + effective 24 October 1945 + UNSC established 24 October 1945 by UN Charter Chapter V Article 23 — formal foundational UNSC framework operational.

key_developments.title: First UNSC meeting + Soviet first veto
key_developments.description: First UNSC meeting 17 January 1946 in London + Soviet Union first veto 16 February 1946 — formal first UNSC meeting + first veto framework operational.

key_developments.title: Korean War + Uniting for Peace + Soviet boycott
key_developments.description: Korean War 25 June 1950 - 27 July 1953 + Soviet boycott of UNSC January-August 1950 + Uniting for Peace UN General Assembly Resolution 377(V) of 3 November 1950 — formal Korean War + Uniting for Peace framework operational.

key_developments.title: China-PRC-replaced-ROC-Taiwan
key_developments.description: UN General Assembly Resolution 2758 of 25 October 1971 — formal China-PRC-replaced-ROC-Taiwan framework operational.

key_developments.title: Russia replaced Soviet Union UNSC seat
key_developments.description: Russia replaced Soviet Union UNSC seat 24 December 1991 following Soviet dissolution 26 December 1991 — formal Russia-replaced-USSR-UNSC-seat framework operational.

key_developments.title: First UNSC Summit + High-Level Open Debates
key_developments.description: First UNSC Summit held 31 January 1992 — formal UNSC Summit framework operational.

key_developments.title: World Summit Outcome Document + R2P + Children and Armed Conflict + UN Security Council Resolution 1612
key_developments.description: World Summit Outcome Document adopted 24 October 2005 + R2P Responsibility to Protect 2005 + UN Security Council Resolution 1612 of 26 July 2005 — formal foundational R2P + Children and Armed Conflict Six Grave Violations framework operational.

key_developments.title: Russia-vetoes Crimea + Donbas
key_developments.description: Russia-vetoes Crimea 15 March 2014 + Donbas + Russia-Crimea-annexation 18 March 2014 — formal Russia-vetoes-Crimea framework operational.

key_developments.title: Russia-Ukraine + Veto Initiative + Russia-vetoes
key_developments.description: Russia invaded Ukraine 24 February 2022 + Russia-vetoes Russia-Ukraine UN Security Council Resolution 25 February 2022 + UN General Assembly Resolution 76/262 Veto Initiative of 26 April 2022 + Russia-suspended Human Rights Council 7 April 2022 — formal Russia-Ukraine + Veto Initiative + Russia-suspended-HRC framework operational.

key_developments.title: Israel-Hamas-Gaza war + US-vetoes-Gaza-ceasefire
key_developments.description: Israel-Hamas-Gaza war 7 October 2023 onwards + US-vetoes-Gaza-ceasefire UNSC Resolutions 2023-2024 framework + UNSC Resolution 2728 of 25 March 2024 demand-immediate-ceasefire-Ramadan adopted US-abstained framework — formal Israel-Hamas-Gaza + US-vetoes-Gaza-ceasefire framework operational.

key_developments.title: Pact for the Future + UNSC reform commitments
key_developments.description: Pact for the Future adopted 22 September 2024 at UN Summit of the Future New York including UNSC reform commitments framework — formal foundational Pact for the Future + UNSC reform commitments framework operational.

key_developments.title: UN80 Reform Process + UNSC reform
key_developments.description: UN80 Reform Process 2025-2026 framework via UN Secretary-General Guterres-UN80-Initiative March 2025 + UNSC reform 80-year-anniversary 2025 framework — formal UN80 Reform + UNSC reform framework operational.

structural_findings: UN Security Council operates a supranational global binding-decision-making framework — places UNSC in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating on formal international-peace-and-security framework + foundational collective-security framework with five-permanent-members-veto framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating on formal international-peace-and-security framework + foundational collective-security framework with five-permanent-members-veto framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal binding-resolutions framework under UN Charter Chapter VII framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal P5 Permanent Five US + UK + France + Russia + China veto framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal China-PRC-replaced-ROC-Taiwan 1971 framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal Russia-vetoes 2022-2026 framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal US-vetoes-Gaza-ceasefire-2023-2026 framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal UNSC-reform-Pact-for-Future 2024 framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal Veto-Initiative-2022 framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal Article 27(3) procedural-substantive distinction framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal Korea peacekeeping-since-1950 framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal Article 51 self-defense framework + only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal Pillar 3 Responsibility to Protect R2P 2005 framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal UN Charter Chapter VII binding-resolutions framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal binding-resolutions framework under UN Charter Chapter VII framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal P5 Permanent Five veto framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal P5 Permanent Five veto framework + most-vetoes-cast: Russia/Soviet Union 158 + US 89 + UK 32 + China 21 + France 18 as of 2026 framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal UNSC reform + Pact for Future 2024 framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-decision-making framework operating formal 80-year-reform-debate framework.
193 UN member state ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — comprehensive universal coverage framework.

1945 UN Charter + UNSC + 1946 first meeting + Soviet first veto + 1950 Korea + Soviet boycott + Uniting for Peace + 1971 China-PRC-replaced-ROC-Taiwan + 1991 Russia-replaced-USSR + 1992 first UNSC Summit + 2005 World Summit + R2P + SC 1612 + 2014 Russia-Crimea + 2022 Russia-Ukraine + Veto Initiative + 2023 Israel-Hamas-Gaza + US-vetoes + 2024 Pact for Future + 2025 UN80 UNSC-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

UNSC + UN Charter Chapter V + Chapter VII + Article 27(3) P5 veto + SC 1612 Children and Armed Conflict + Pact for the Future formal sextet framework with formal SC 1612 Six Grave Violations Against Children PA-intersection framework via UNSC Working Group on Children and Armed Conflict framework.

sources.title: UN Security Council
sources.publisher: UN Department of Political and Peacebuilding Affairs

NOTE: UN Security Council Resolution 1612 of 26 July 2005 Children and Armed Conflict framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Six Grave Violations Against Children: killing-maiming + recruitment-use-of-children + sexual-violence + abduction + attacks-on-schools + denial-of-humanitarian-access framework + UN Secretary-General Annual Report on Children and Armed Conflict framework + UN Security Council Working Group on Children and Armed Conflict framework.
